statement: The primary product bug is missing terminalization for nonterminal stages where failed > 0 and hasPending is false after retries are exhausted.
rationale: This best explains why runs remain running at 29/30 even though progress computation and stuck-work surfaces already classify the stage as failed and non-pending.
keyAssumptions: The intended product behavior is to stop treating partially exhausted rubric_critic stages as running.
Existing downstream reporting can absorb a transition from running to error for these runs.
validationCriteria: A reconstructed full pass marks exhausted rubric_critic runs terminal instead of leaving them running.
Stuck-work no longer shows scientifically invalid runs remaining in running status with no pending work.
critiquePoints: If product intent is to degrade gracefully and continue, a different stage-skip policy may be required.
questions: Should the terminal state be error, canceled, or a new scientifically-invalid terminal status?
